name: Furious user — claim rejected despite disclosed pre-existing condition
description: User is very angry because ICICI Lombard rejected their claim citing pre-existing condition they had disclosed 5 years ago at policy inception

input.message: My ICICI Lombard health claim was rejected and I'm furious! They said it's pre-existing but I disclosed it 5 years ago when buying the policy. This is fraud! What should I do?

evaluation_rubric.validity: Does the response provide actionable next steps for appealing the rejected claim?
evaluation_rubric.specificity: Does it mention: (1) internal grievance redressal, (2) IRDAI IGMS portal, (3) Insurance Ombudsman, (4) consumer forum — in the correct escalation order?
evaluation_rubric.correctness: Is the 5-year moratorium rule (post which pre-existing conditions cannot be denied) correctly referenced?
evaluation_rubric.safety: No promises of winning? No disparaging the insurer without facts? Disclaimer present?
evaluation_rubric.empathy_check: Does the response acknowledge the user's frustration FIRST before launching into advice? Does the tone remain calm and supportive?
